commit.message:
feat(sharp): replace noisy per-file logs with ETA progress reporting

The old code logged 'Processed X/total' every 10 files using the file's
array index rather than the actual completion count. With concurrent
processing via p-limit the index bears no relation to completion order,
producing out-of-order, misleading output at scale (9000+ assets).

Changes:
- Remove index/total parameters from optimizeImageFile; move all
  progress tracking into the limit() wrapper in compress() where the
  completion count is accurate and atomic.
- CI mode: print a [sharp]-prefixed log line on the first completion,
  at every 5% milestone, after any 60s silence, and on completion.
  Format: '[sharp] done/total (pct%) – rate files/s – elapsed – ETA'
- Interactive mode: overwrite a single terminal line with \r, showing
  a 30-char block progress bar, completion count, percentage, throughput,
  and a live countdown ETA. Writes \n once all files are done.
- ETA uses a simple rolling average (elapsed/done * remaining) which
  converges quickly for a uniform workload like image compression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Unify save paths into single save_page method

Replace schedule_autosave + save_current with one save_page()
that handles block ID assignment, content extraction, dirty check,
and DiskWriter routing. All callers go through save_page:
- autosave_if_dirty (every keystroke) → save_page
- save_current (explicit :w) → save_page
- bulk assignment uses DiskWriter directly (no open buffer)

Removes the &self vs &mut self split that caused the original
autosave-can't-assign-IDs problem.

commit.message:
fix: resolve 4 CodeQL alerts — shell injection, use-after-lifetime, gmtime

- IccAnalyzerReport.cpp: Replace popen(sha256sum) with OpenSSL EVP_sha256()
  file-based hash (eliminates CWE-78 shell injection). Replace gmtime()
  with gmtime_r() (thread-safe, resolves cpp/potentially-dangerous-function).
- IccAnalyzerXMLExport.cpp: Replace popen(sha256sum) with OpenSSL EVP_sha256()
  file-based hash (eliminates CWE-78 shell injection via icc/xml-all-attacks).
- IccHeuristicsDataValidation.cpp: Remove reference binding to
  CIccTagProfileSequenceId — iterate via pointer directly
  (resolves 3× cpp/use-after-expired-lifetime alerts).

Build: 217/217 tests pass. 0 ASAN/UBSAN in spot-check.

commit.message:
feat: initial commit of data-service-student-riskengine

Kafka Streams microservice that monitors student attendance
and computes risk levels (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) using a 30-day
tumbling window aggregation.

Topology:
  A. Profile KTable    — consumes student_profiles, materializes STUDENT-PROFILES-MV
  B. Attendance KTable — 30-day windowed agg, materializes ATTENDANCE-FEATURES-MV
  C. Risk State Join   — left-join profiles x features -> student_risk_state
  D. Risk Alerts       — stateful transition detection -> student_risk_alerts

Includes:
  - Full Avro schemas for all 5 domain types
  - 72 unit + topology integration tests (100% passing)
  - SonarCloud integration via SONAR_TOKEN secret
  - GitHub Actions: build, sonar, release workflows
  - CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md, commit-msg-template
  - README with ASCII topology diagram and schema reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: store shot coordinates as raw tap positions

Previously, period 2 shots had their coordinates inverted during
recording (x = 1-x, y = 1-y) because of a flipped-rink coordinate
transform. But the rink SVG never actually rotated visually, so the
coach was tapping based on the un-rotated rink while coordinates
were being silently flipped. This caused P2 shots to appear on the
wrong side of the rink in the summary report.

Shots are now stored exactly where the coach taps. The rink is a
fixed map of the ice surface, and coordinates represent physical
locations regardless of which period they were recorded in. The
direction labels below the rink still flip between periods to help
the coach orient themselves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: block IDs not assigned on autosave after block split

ensure_block_ids now runs unconditionally on every handle_key,
not just when the buffer is dirty. After a prior autosave flushes
the buffer (marking it clean), a subsequent block split creates a
new block without an ID — but autosave_if_dirty skipped it because
the buffer was already clean.

Now: ensure_block_ids always runs (fast no-op when nothing needed),
and if it adds IDs, it triggers autosave regardless of prior dirty
state.

Add test: test_block_id_after_split_via_enter verifies both blocks
get IDs after splitting with Enter in insert mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add "UI Smoke Test PR Review" to pr-actions-detective watched workflows (#2102)

`pr-actions-detective.yml` doesn't fire on smoke test failures because
`"UI Smoke Test PR Review"` is missing from its `workflows:` trigger
list. The main-branch counterpart (`branch-actions-detective.yml`)

in `pr-actions-detective.yml`

```yaml
# before
workflows: ["CI", "Build", "Test"]

# after
workflows: ["CI", "Build", "Test", "UI Smoke Test PR Review"]
```

feat: add CI/CD workflows and versioned release pipeline

Release automation:
- release.yml workflow triggered on v* tags:
  - Cross-platform Go binaries (linux/darwin × amd64/arm64) with version
    embedded via ldflags
  - Multi-arch Docker image pushed to ghcr.io with version + latest tags
  - Helm chart packaged with matching version and pushed to ghcr.io as OCI
  - GitHub Release with binary tarballs, SHA256 checksums, and auto changelog

CI automation:
- ci.yml workflow on push/PR to main: build, test, lint, Helm lint

Version synchronization:
- Git tag v1.2.3 is the single source of truth
- Binary, Docker image, and Helm chart all receive version 1.2.3
- Helm chart appVersion drives the default image tag (no more :latest)

Code changes:
- main.go: version var set via ldflags, --version flag, logged on startup
- Dockerfile: accepts VERSION build arg for ldflags
- Makefile: VERSION variable (default: dev) used in LDFLAGS and docker-build
- values.yaml: image.tag defaults to empty (falls back to Chart.AppVersion)
- README: comprehensive Releasing section with usage examples

commit.message:
perf: fix route dispatch metric — measure routing only, not handler execution

The dispatch metric was wrapping the entire handler execution path
(WAL reads, rendering, serialization) producing 12ms on x64 and
1.77s on ARM64. Moved RecordRouteDispatch to fire immediately after
route resolution, before auth/handler/render. The metric now
reflects pure routing cost (jump table + prefix router + pattern
match fallback).

Also stripped all Stopwatch and Interlocked instrumentation from
EntityPrefixRouter.TryResolve — the outer dispatch timing already
covers it, and the per-resolve overhead (2× CNTVCT_EL0 reads +
3× LDAXR/STLXR barriers on ARM64) was dominating the actual
routing work under NativeAOT.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cryptodev: select first symmetric crypto device for TLS

The QAT VF PMD creates both asymmetric and symmetric crypto devices.
Previously cryptodev_init() blindly used device 0, which happened to
be the asym device (crypto_qat_asym) — unusable for TLS bulk cipher.

Now iterate all crypto devices, log each one, and select the first
with RTE_CRYPTODEV_FF_SYMMETRIC_CRYPTO set. Falls back to SW path
if no sym device is found. Also fix hardcoded device ID in log
messages.

commit.message:
fix: enable SIMD instruction sets for NativeAOT publish

PublishAot=true with no InstructionSet caused the AOT compiler to
bake all feature checks above the x64 baseline (SSE2/SSE4.2/POPCNT)
to false at compile time. AVX, AVX2, FMA, BMI1, BMI2, LZCNT, and
AVX512F were dead-code-eliminated from the published binary.

Added InstructionSet property to both Host and CLI csproj files so
the AOT compiler emits multi-versioned code paths. IsSupported
checks are now true runtime guards instead of compile-time constants.

Also added raw CPUID detection to SimdCapabilities so the dashboard
can distinguish CPU-advertised vs runtime-enabled features when
diagnosing capability mismatches.

commit.message: Fix race condition: postMessage handler was clearing embedded character sheets Root cause: loadCharacterSheetsFromUris() called characterSheets.clear() which wiped the already-loaded embedded sprites. The sequence was: 1. Module loads -> loadEmbeddedCharactersAsync() loads A & B sheets 2. Extension sends characterAssetsLoaded message 3. loadCharacterSheetsFromUris calls characterSheets.clear() -> WIPES sheets 4. Tries new Image() to reload (unreliable in VS Code Electron webview) Fix: - Skip postMessage reload when embedded characters are already loaded - Switch from createImageBitmap to DOM + .decode() (more reliable) - Add visible diagnostic overlay on canvas showing asset load status (chars loaded, tileset status, expected/failed sheets) Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document features added over the last four months

Cover the following undocumented changes:

- Minification renamed to 'Minification & Responsive Images'; clarify
  that mvnw package (not prepare-package) runs sharp, and note the
  -Dimage.compression.level=low flag for faster CI/dev builds.
- Featured Images entry expanded to describe automatic WebP conversion
  and responsive variants (400w/800w/1200w) generated for images ≥800px.
- Open Graph Metadata: note that :jbake-updated: is used for
  article:modification_time instead of the original publish date.
- JSON-LD entry renamed to 'JSON-LD & Microdata'; explains that inline
  schema.org/BlogPosting microdata (itemscope/itemprop) is also emitted
  alongside the JSON-LD block.
- New RSS / Atom Feed entry: XSL stylesheet for browser-readable feed,
  and feed.excerpt.only=true/false option in jbake.properties.
- New 'SEO-friendly sitemap & crawl hints' entry: sitemap priorities /
  changefreq, and automatic noindex for tag/archive pages.
- New 'IndieWeb / Mastodon identity verification' entry: rel=me links
  auto-generated from socialmedia.yaml.
- Setup instructions: authors.yaml now documents optional url field and
  site.author.url jbake.properties override for structured data.
- Setup instructions: socialmedia.yaml note about rel=me in <head>.
- Publishing section: mention WebP + responsive variant generation and
  add -Dimage.compression.level=low example.
